options: A. Inline & Discrete (minimal additions in existing surfaces, autonomous classification — no user-trigger buttons)
B. Drift Hub (dedicated tab + activity rail + 'Run assessment' button)
C. Diff-Forward / Conversational (inline diff cards with Accept/Surface/Ignore buttons)
decision_log.choice: A. Inline & Discrete (minimal additions in existing surfaces, autonomous classification — no user-trigger buttons)
decision_log.rationale: User wants the system to auto-resolve drift on the next tick (agent classifies, agent acts) — drift detection and classification are NOT user actions. UI surfaces are passive observers that show what was detected and what the agent decided, not control surfaces with Assess/Accept/Surface/Ignore buttons. Implies: no "Run now ↻" button on the drift banner; no Accept/Surface/Ignore on inline diff cards; the four classification outcomes are agent-decided, not user-selected.
